claim: Bolt.diy (open-source bolt.new) is now official via partnership with StackBlitz, enabling teams to run locally or in the cloud with full control over LLM choice.
actionable: Use bolt.diy instead of commercial bolt.new if you need local hosting, custom prompts per LLM, or prefer open-source; deploy via Cloudflare Pages.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Different LLMs need different prompts: Claude 3.5 Sonnet requires a specific prompt, but Qwen, Llama 3.3, and Gemini perform poorly with the same prompt and need optimization.
actionable: When switching LLMs, tune the system prompt for each model; don't assume one prompt works across all LLMs in production.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Prompt library is a critical feature for multi-LLM systems: store optimized prompts per LLM family (Qwen, Llama, Gemini, Claude) and make them adjustable/extendable.
actionable: Build a prompt library keyed by LLM family; tag prompts with tested model versions and performance metrics.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: File walking and diffs are essential for avoiding redundant file rewrites; agents should patch files rather than regenerating entire files each turn.
actionable: Implement diff/patch-based APIs for agents instead of full file replacement; this reduces token waste and improves edit locality.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Plugin system (extension marketplace) allows contributors to extend bolt.diy without modifying core; this prevents feature bloat and makes features opt-in.
actionable: Design AI coding tools with plugin architecture so users can opt-in to features (document upload, reasoning models, Supabase integration) without UI clutter.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Smaller LLMs (7B params) perform poorly as single agents; mixture-of-experts approach (multiple small models coordinating) could unlock better results at lower cost.
actionable: For resource-constrained environments, experiment with multi-agent architectures using smaller models rather than single large models.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Open-source contribution is incentivized via partnership cash prizes and events with StackBlitz to fund community contributors instead of relying on crowdfunding.
actionable: If maintaining an open-source AI tool, partner with companies to fund contributor rewards rather than relying on individual sponsorships.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Kanban board (Trello/GitHub Projects style) for organizing high-priority PRs and backlog improves team visibility and task prioritization in open-source projects.
actionable: Use a public Kanban board for open-source project management; expose high-priority items and roadmap publicly.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Cloudflare Pages is the recommended deployment platform for bolt.diy; deployment includes auth flow, read-only API access, and proper environment variable handling.
actionable: Deploy bolt.diy on Cloudflare Pages; follow the comprehensive guide with authentication and API key setup.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding

claim: Experimental system prompt feature allows testing new prompts without affecting stable builds; this is the pattern for prompt optimization in multi-LLM systems.
actionable: Implement experimental vs. default prompt flags in your LLM system; let users opt-in to experimental prompts for specific LLM families.
source_title: bolt.diy: The Future of Open Source AI Coding
